protocol: solo Tier-A adversarial Lane-V (5 independent non-author verifiers, read-only, model=sonnet)
overall_note: 4 GO + 1 NITS; zero FAIL/CRITICAL/MAJOR. Only actionable NIT = doc-anchor :338->:344 (fixed). Two fail-safe-totality NITs confirmed test-API-only + unreachable on the bus + not-a-regression.

verifiers.tests_run: env -u GIT_INDEX_FILE .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/test_threeway_reducer.py tests/unit/test_threeway_predicate.py -v → 82 passed in 0.05s\nenv -u GIT_INDEX_FILE .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/ -q → 3205 passed, 1 skipped in 220s\n/tmp/test_mutation_probe.py (monkeypatch is_aborted to bare set-membership) → 5 mutant probes PASSED (exploit confirmed), 5 real-code probes PASSED (fix blocks all 5)
verifiers.summary: Verified authority/forge lens against the candidate_aborted no-authority fix. The fix replaces bare set-membership in is_aborted (threeway/reducer.py:95-119) with a read-time authority check: an abort is effective only if the candidate_id's bound-pair executing_coordinator (from the overseer-only assignment) is among the aborting seats. Five adversarial paths were probed by monkeypatching is_aborted back to the pre-fix form: operator-signed abort, coordinator2 (cross-pair) abort, ci-signed abort, merge-gate-signed abort, and a forged-assignment-then-operator-abort — every one returned is_aborted=True under the mutant (exploit confirmed non-vacuous) and is_aborted=False under the real fix (all blocked). The assignment fold at reducer.py:377-380 is overseer-only at record time (_AUTHORIZED_SINGLETON_SEAT["assignment"]="overseer"), so a forged non-overseer assignment is dropped before it can redirect abort authority. The overseer-cannot-abort result (is_aborted=False for an overseer-signed abort) is correct-as-designed per user decision 2026-06-23 (executing-coordinator-only scope; DD-4 in ADR-059). The rework.py raw-abort counter is intentionally deferred C1 Part 2, is currently unwired (no callers in gate.py, loop.py, or predicate.py), and does not affect the abort-authority gate. ARCHITECTURE.md line references (:69, :95, :159, :338) are accurate. Full unit suite: 3205 passed, 1 skipped, 0 failures.

env -u GIT_INDEX_FILE .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/test_threeway_reducer.py tests/unit/test_threeway_predicate.py -q → 82 passed in 0.06s

Mutation probe (authority check removed, /tmp/muttest_pkg copy): 5 FAILED / 77 passed — tests test_forged_abort_from_non_coordinator_is_ignored, test_cross_pair_coordinator_abort_is_ignored, test_forged_assignment_cannot_redirect_abort_authority, test_forged_abort_from_operator_does_not_reject, test_cross_pair_coordinator_abort_does_not_reject all went RED, confirming non-vacuous.

Ordering probe script (/tmp/ordering_probe.py, 8 scenarios): All 8 passed.
verifiers.summary: I verified all ordering/read-time edge cases independently. The fix correctly resolves abort authority at READ time (is_aborted, reducer.py:95–122) using _aborted_by: dict[candidate_id → set(seats)] populated unconditionally at fold time (reducer.py:344), then cross-referenced at query time against assignment(ns).payload['executing_coordinator']. Every ordering scenario — abort before assignment, abort with no candidate fact, two overseer reassignments (latest wins), revoked assignment (→ False), wrong-pair assignment (→ False), un-namespaced id (→ False), multiple non-coordinator aborters (→ False), and namespace derived from id not from payload — was probed and passed. Mutation (removing `coordinator in seats`) turned 5 tests RED, proving non-vacuousness. The single live call site is predicate.py:49; rework.py counts raw aborts but is confirmed UNWIRED (zero production callers). No stale _aborted_candidates references remain.

findings.claim: is_aborted() can raise TypeError if called directly with an unhashable candidate_id (e.g., a list) — self._aborted_by.get([list]) raises TypeError.
findings.evidence: Probe /tmp/probe_failsafe.py P1: `state.is_aborted(['A','c1'])` raised `TypeError: unhashable type: 'list'`. HOWEVER this is NOT reachable from the production bus path: (a) run_gate/gate.py line ~165 guards: `if not isinstance(candidate_id, str): return GateResult('REJECTED', ...)` before any call to evaluate(); (b) evaluate() is the only production call site (predicate.py:49); (c) evaluate() sits inside run_gate's outer `try/except Exception` (gate.py ~line 189-240), so even a hypothetical raise would yield GateResult('REJECTED', ...) not an uncaught crash. Confirmed by /tmp/probe2_reachability.py R1: run_gate(['A','c1']) returned GateResult(outcome='REJECTED', ...) without raising.

findings.claim: is_aborted() raises AttributeError if assignment.payload is mutated to a non-dict post-reduce (a.payload.get(...) on a list raises AttributeError).
findings.evidence: Probe /tmp/probe_failsafe.py P5: forcibly setting event.payload to a list via object.__setattr__ then calling is_aborted raised AttributeError. NOT reachable from bus: well_formed() (envelope.py:141) requires isinstance(ev.payload, dict) and filters malformed events BEFORE the fold. Confirmed by /tmp/probe2_reachability.py R2: well_formed(non-dict payload)=False. No production code mutates stored event payloads after reduce(). Both raises are TEST-API-only; the production run_gate call path is fully protected.

Non-vacuous RED proof (mutation probe /tmp/mutation_probe.py — pre-fix is_aborted = bare set-membership):
  test_forged_abort_from_non_coordinator_is_ignored: is_aborted=True → RED (test expects False)
  test_cross_pair_coordinator_abort_is_ignored: is_aborted=True → RED (test expects False)
  test_abort_without_assignment_is_ignored: is_aborted=True → RED (test expects False)
  test_authorized_abort_is_recorded: is_aborted=True → GREEN (still passes, correct)

Fail-safe probe /tmp/probe_failsafe.py:
  P1 TypeError from is_aborted([list]): reachability blocked by run_gate isinstance guard (R1 PASS)
  P2 is_aborted(None): safely returns False
  P3 reduce(candidate_aborted cid=None): safely stores under None key, is_aborted(None)=False
  P4 non-str executing_coordinator (list/dict/int/None/True): isinstance guard returns False, no raise
  P5 non-dict payload (direct mutation): AttributeError — unreachable from bus (well_formed filter)
  P6 _pair_namespace(list/dict/int/None/''/no-colon): all return None safely
  P7 fold with unhashable cid via __setattr__: well_formed=False, fold never reached
  P8 all str candidate_id combos across 4 state variants: no raise
  P9 assignment keyed by None (missing pair in payload): is_aborted('A:c1')=False, no raise
verifiers.summary: The new is_aborted() and its fold branch are fail-safe on all bus-reachable inputs. The five-layer defense is intact: (1) well_formed() rejects unhashable/non-str candidate_id and non-dict payload before the fold; (2) run_gate's isinstance(candidate_id, str) guard blocks non-str ids before evaluate(); (3) all five early-exit False paths in is_aborted (no seats entry, None namespace, None assignment, None coordinator, non-str coordinator) handle every plausible edge case without raising; (4) the fold's surrounding try/except catches any residual TypeError/AttributeError; (5) run_gate's outer except Exception catches anything that reaches evaluate(). Two theoretical raises (unhashable cid direct-call, mutated payload direct-call) exist only as test-API surface — both are multiply blocked before the production bus path. Three new authority-check tests are non-vacuous: all go RED when the coordinator guard is removed. Full suite: 3205 passed, 0 failures.

findings.claim: The namespace None-guard in is_aborted (reducer.py:112-114) is defense-in-depth, not the primary gate.
findings.evidence: Mutation 2 removed lines 113-114 ('if ns is None: return False') and all 82 tests still passed. The primary guard is `self.assignment(ns)` returning None when ns is None (dict key miss), which fires the `if a is None: return False` branch. The explicit ns-None check is correct belt-and-suspenders but not load-bearing — the ARCHITECTURE.md claim 'Fail-safe: no ... namespace ... -> False' is satisfied by the assignment lookup, not exclusively by the explicit guard. This is harmless and not a defect.

findings.claim: rework.py:10 counts raw (unauthorized) aborts — the deferred C1 Part 2 — is NOT wired into gate.py/run_gate.
findings.evidence: grep -n 'rework' /Users/hyungkoookkim/Content/threeway/gate.py returned no output. ARCHITECTURE.md explicitly discloses this: 'currently UNWIRED'. Not a regression introduced by this fix, and scoped to a deferred item.

Key pins (production tree):

  test_forged_abort_from_operator_does_not_reject PASSED — operator forged abort now MERGEable (DoS closed)

verifiers.summary: The fix replaces _aborted_candidates (bare set) with _aborted_by (dict[candidate_id -> set(signer-seats)]) and adds read-time authority resolution in is_aborted: abort is effective only when the candidate's pair-namespaced executing_coordinator (from the overseer-only assignment event) is among the aborting seats. The fix exclusively NARROWS: a forged (non-coordinator) abort now returns False where it previously returned True, and a real authorized abort still returns True. No path outside the abort mechanism was touched — revoke, brief_superseded, and assignment-authority branches are unchanged (confirmed by diff line-by-line grep). Mutation probing confirmed non-vacuity: stripping the authority check flips all four forged-abort pins RED. The rework.py raw-count circuit-breaker remains unwired and was explicitly disclosed as deferred; it is not a regression introduced by this fix.

findings.claim: ARCHITECTURE.md cites reducer.py:338 as the fold write site, but the actual _aborted_by.setdefault(...) write is at line 344; line 338 is the elif k == 'candidate_aborted': branch header.
findings.evidence: grep -n '_aborted_by.setdefault' threeway/reducer.py -> line 344; sed -n '338p' threeway/reducer.py -> 'elif k == "candidate_aborted":'. The ARCHITECTURE.md new paragraph reads '(_fold_, threeway/reducer.py:338)'. Both lines are within the same branch so the anchor is not broken, but the prose says 'fold' and points to the branch header rather than the write line.

verifiers.tests_run: env -u GIT_INDEX_FILE .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/ -k threeway -q --tb=no -> 404 passed, 1 skipped, 2803 deselected (two independent runs: 108.88s + 137.20s). Mutation probe: /tmp/test_mutant_probe.py with monkeypatched EffectiveState.is_aborted replaced by 'return True for any abort fact' -> 5 FAILED (forged-operator, cross-pair-coord, no-assignment, unnamespaced, forged-assignment-redirect) + 1 PASSED (authorized abort control) -> pins are NON-VACUOUS. env -u GIT_INDEX_FILE .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/unit/test_threeway_reducer.py tests/unit/test_threeway_predicate.py -v -> 82 passed (all PASSED, 0 FAILED).
verifiers.summary: The fix correctly replaces bare set-membership is_aborted with a read-time authority check: _aborted_by records all abort signer-seats; is_aborted resolves the candidate_id's pair namespace, looks up the overseer-assigned executing_coordinator, and returns True only if that coordinator is in the aborting set. Five independent forged-abort scenarios (operator, cross-pair coordinator, no-assignment, unnamespaced id, forged-assignment redirect) all go RED under the authority-stripped mutant and pass GREEN on real code, proving non-vacuousness. All 8 new reducer tests and 3 new predicate tests are correctly pointed at authorized-abort semantics, not weakened. rework.py should_escalate/rework_count have zero production callers so the C1 Part 2 deferral introduces no live regression. The single NIT is a cosmetic ARCHITECTURE.md anchor off-by-6 (cites :338 the elif branch header; the write is at :344).
